topic: Meta unveils Muse Image and Muse Video AI models
headline: META UNVEILS MUSE IMAGE AND MUSE VIDEO AI MODELS, REVOLUTIONIZING VISUAL CONTENT CREATION
summary: Meta has launched Muse Image, an AI model that can generate images, and announced an early preview of Muse Video, a video generation model. Muse Image is available to users, while Muse Video will be available to creators in the coming months. The models are part of Meta's expanded generative AI tools across its apps.

summary_sources.title: Meta's latest AI model can generate images, websites and even QR codes | Mint
Meta expands generative AI tools with Muse Image rollout
Meta unveils new AI model as it tries to catch up with Google and OpenAI after spending billions | Technology News - The Indian Express
Why is Meta’s Muse Image AI feature facing a backlash? - The Hindu

sections.paragraph: Meta has launched two new AI models, Muse Image and Muse Video, with Muse Image being made available to users immediately. Muse Image is described as Meta's most advanced image generation model, capable of following user instructions, making edits with precision, and composing images from multiple reference shots. The model can also search the web for real-time information, write and execute code, and refine its own outputs. Additionally, Muse Image can interpret complex prompts, use photos as inputs, and allow users to edit generated images directly through sketches or annotations. However, the feature has faced backlash, with some criticizing its ability to turn publicly available photos and other people's media into AI content without their consent. Muse Image is available through the Meta AI app, the meta.ai website, and is rolling out to Instagram Stories in the US and WhatsApp in select countries.

sections.paragraph: Meta has unveiled a new artificial intelligence model called Muse Spark, marking the first launch under its new AI division. The company has spent billions building this division, aiming to become more competitive in the AI space, which is currently dominated by Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The development of Muse Spark was completed in just nine months, with Meta's Superintelligence Labs rebuilding its AI stack from the ground up. This initial model is designed to be small and fast, yet capable of reasoning through complex questions in science, math, and health. The launch of Muse Spark is seen as a significant step for Meta, with the next generation of the model already in development. The move is part of Meta's efforts to catch up with its competitors in the AI sector.

sources.title: Meta unveils new AI model as it tries to catch up with Google and OpenAI after spending billions | Technology News - The Indian Express

sections.paragraph: The growing use of AI-generated imagery has significant social implications, particularly when it comes to the safety and privacy of children. Parents are being warned not to publicly share images of their children online due to the risk of these images being used to create child sexual abuse material, with over 8,000 AI-generated images and videos identified in 2025, a 14% increase from the previous year. Schools are also being cautioned against posting pupils' images on their websites and social media pages, as this can expose them to potential identity fraud, harassment, and AI exploitation. Experts emphasize that these are not hypothetical threats, but real concerns that require informed decision-making. The issue is not limited to child safety, as AI-generated content is also being used to manipulate and deceive in other contexts, further eroding public trust. As the use of AI-generated imagery becomes more widespread, it is essential to be aware of the potential risks and take steps to mitigate them.

sources.title: Parents warned not to publicly share children’s images amid AI abuse risks
Trump's use of AI images pushes new boundaries, further eroding public trust | AP News
Dangers of putting pupils’ images on school websites | Internet safety | The Guardian

sections.paragraph: The rollout of Meta's Muse Image, a generative AI tool, has sparked a geopolitical backlash, with Hollywood talent union SAG-AFTRA condemning the feature for allowing users to turn publicly available photos and other people's media into AI content without their consent. Muse Image, launched by Meta Superintelligence Labs, is integrated into Meta's AI chatbot and can interpret complex prompts, use photos as inputs, and enable users to edit generated images. The feature is set to power over 30 new AI effects for Instagram Stories. However, its launch on July 7 has been met with widespread criticism, particularly regarding the issue of consent and the potential for misuse of users' media. The controversy surrounding Muse Image highlights the geopolitical implications of emerging technologies and the need for companies to consider the potential consequences of their innovations. The backlash against Meta's feature has sparked a debate about the responsible development and deployment of AI technologies.

consistency_check.notes: The Business Impact angle mentions 'Muse Spark', while the Summary and other angles mention 'Muse Image' and 'Muse Video', indicating a direct contradiction in the naming of the AI models.
